Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, classify throttling, transient transport, server, permanent, cancellation, conflict, and ambiguous write outcomes with bounded attempts, time, delay, and bytes.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Retry Classification Budget model · Theory Retry Classifi…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: All 5xx and network failures are safe to retry without reconciliation. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
